assertions.title: Move History in Algebraic Notation
assertions.reason: The move history rendered SAN correctly (including castling) and auto-scrolled to the newest row after 16 full moves.

steps.action: Start a fresh Easy game and play 16 full moves through the board UI.
steps.expected: History panel shows SAN rows and keeps the latest move visible.
steps.observed: History showed `1. e4 e5 ... 6. O-O O-O ... 16. Rab1 Bxe1`, and the scroll area stayed pinned to the bottom.

evidence.historyObservation: `scrollTop` was 561 and `scrollHeight - clientHeight` was also 561, so the latest row (16. Rab1 Bxe1) was in view.
evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

assertions.title: Captured Pieces Display
assertions.reason: After White played `gxh5`, the captured-pieces panel immediately showed one black pawn under White's captures, matching the SAN and board state.

steps.action: Play `1. g4 e5 2. Bg2 h5 3. gxh5` and wait for the engine reply `...Qg5`.
steps.expected: The capture should appear in the captured-pieces area and agree with move history.
steps.observed: History showed `3. gxh5 Qg5`, `black pawn` appeared under White's captures, and the h5 pawn was gone from the board.

evidence.captureObservation: Capture labels were `["black pawn"]`, which matched the only capture in the line.
evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

assertions.title: Undo Move
assertions.reason: Undo removed the last player/engine pair, restored the captured pawn, removed the capture from history, and the control was disabled again after a full reset.

steps.action: From `1. g4 e5 2. Bg2 h5 3. gxh5 Qg5`, click Undo.
steps.expected: Board, history, and captures revert to the position after `2...h5`.
steps.observed: History reverted to `1. g4 e5 2. Bg2 h5`, the black pawn returned to h5, and the captured-pieces panel emptied.

steps.action: Click New Game and inspect Undo.
steps.expected: Undo is disabled or a no-op at the starting position.
steps.observed: After reset, Undo was disabled while move count was 0.

evidence.historyObservation: Move count dropped from 6 ply to 4 ply after Undo, and the last move changed from `Qg5` back to `h5`.
evidence.captureObservation: White's capture list changed from `[black pawn]` back to empty after Undo.
evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

assertions.title: New Game Reset
assertions.reason: New Game restored the starting layout, cleared history and captures, reset the status to White's turn, and returned evaluation to the neutral opening state.

steps.action: From an active game, click New Game.
steps.expected: Board resets, history empties, captures clear, evaluation returns to neutral, and White is to move.
steps.observed: Board returned to the initial 32-piece setup, history showed `0 full moves`, captures showed `None yet`, evaluation was `+0.1`/centered opening bar, and status was `Your move.`

evidence.historyObservation: `Moves played` returned to 0 and `Last move` returned to `None yet`.
evidence.captureObservation: Both capture groups showed `None yet` after reset.
evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

assertions.title: Post-Game Undo
assertions.reason: After the game-over state from `1. g4 e5 2. f3 Qh4#`, Undo restored the prior position and did not trigger any replacement engine move.

steps.action: Reach checkmate with `1. g4 e5 2. f3 Qh4#`.
steps.observed: Overlay showed `Checkmate — Claudefish wins.` with move history ending in `Qh4#`.

steps.expected: Review position reappears without an automatic engine response.
steps.observed: The game-over overlay disappeared, history reverted to `1. g4 e5`, move count dropped from 4 to 2, and engine state stayed `Ready for the next move.`

evidence.historyObservation: Undo removed the final move pair rather than leaving the checkmate position locked.
evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

assertions.title: Undo and Divergent Continuation
assertions.reason: After undoing the `gxh5/Qg5` pair, a different white move (`Nf3`) produced a different engine continuation (`...Nc6`), so the old line was discarded correctly.

steps.action: Play `1. g4 e5 2. Bg2 h5 3. gxh5 Qg5`.
steps.expected: Six half-moves are present before undo.
steps.observed: Move history reached 3 full moves with White's capture recorded.

steps.action: Undo once, then play `Nf3` instead of `gxh5`.
steps.expected: Board reverts and the engine replies to the new branch only.
steps.observed: Undo restored the position after `2...h5`; the new branch became `3. Nf3 Nc6` instead of reusing `...Qg5`.

evidence.historyObservation: Pre-undo line: `1. g4 e5 2. Bg2 h5 3. gxh5 Qg5`; post-undo branch: `1. g4 e5 2. Bg2 h5 3. Nf3 Nc6`.
evidence.captureObservation: The temporary `black pawn` capture disappeared on undo and stayed absent on the divergent continuation.
evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

assertions.title: Multiple Sequential Games
assertions.reason: Three consecutive Easy games stayed stable: game 1 completed, game 2 continued through 10 full moves, and game 3 continued through 4 full moves with consistent response times and no browser warnings.

steps.action: Game 1: complete `1. g4 e5 2. f3 Qh4#`.
steps.observed: Game-over overlay appeared after 2 full moves.

steps.action: Game 2: start a new game and play 10 full moves.
steps.expected: Engine remains responsive through a longer second game.
steps.observed: History reached `10 full moves` with no console or memory warnings.

steps.action: Game 3: start another new game and play 4 full moves.
steps.expected: Third game behaves normally as well.
steps.observed: Third game reached `4 full moves` and stayed interactive with normal replies.

responseTimeComparison.observation: Average reply time increased by only ~11.5ms from game 1 to game 3, with all measured replies staying in a narrow 363-381ms band.

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR activity; local localhost:3001 page/asset traffic only.

frictions.description: When the game-over overlay is visible, the page contains two visible `New Game` buttons (main controls plus overlay), so automation had to target the overlay button explicitly before starting the next sequential game.
frictions.resolution: Targeted the overlay's `New Game` button directly and continued with a fresh in-browser game state.

summary: Tested 7 assigned assertions for history/undo/reset flows: 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
